content: I'll split this bead into 5 focused child beads, chained sequentially. Let me create them:

args.command:
br create "Add pose-detection entry to github-eventsource.yml" "Add pose-detection EventSource entry to k8s/iad-ci/argo-events/github-eventsource.yml in jedarden/declarative-config.

Specification:
- EventSource: github-webhooks (ns argo-events)
- owner: jedarden
- names: [pose-detection]
- webhook endpoint: /pose-detection
- port: 12000
- url: https://webhooks-ci.ardenone.com
- events: [push]
- apiToken: from github-webhook-secret (key: token)
- webhookSecret: from github-webhook-secret (key: webhook-secret)
- insecure: false
- active: true
- contentType: json

Acceptance criteria:
1. github-eventsource.yml has pose-detection entry under github-webhooks EventSource
2. File is committed and pushed to declarative-config
3. Changes are visible in git log

Use palm-reading entry as a reference pattern."
args.description: Create first child bead: eventsource entry

output:
Exit code 2
error: unexpected argument 'Add pose-detection entry to github-eventsource.yml' found

Usage: br create [OPTIONS] --title <TITLE>

For more information, try '--help'.

args.command:
br create "Create pose-detection-sensor.yml" "Create pose-detection-sensor.yml in k8s/iad-ci/argo-events/ in jedarden/declarative-config, modeled on palm-reading-sensor.yml.

Specification:
- Sensor: pose-detection-sensor (ns argo-events)
- Dependency: eventSourceName=github-webhooks, eventName=pose-detection
- Filters:
  * headers.X-Github-Event == push
  * body.ref == refs/heads/main
  * auto-bump cascade guard (exclude ci: auto-bump commits from jedarden)
- Trigger: argoWorkflow submit
  * generateName: pose-detection-build-
  * workflowTemplateRef: pose-detection-build
  * params: git-repo=jedarden/pose-detection, branch=main

Acceptance criteria:
1. pose-detection-sensor.yml created with correct spec
2. File is committed and pushed to declarative-config
3. File exists in repo"
args.description: Create second child bead: sensor file

output:
Exit code 2
error: unexpected argument 'Create pose-detection-sensor.yml' found

Usage: br create [OPTIONS] --title <TITLE>

For more information, try '--help'.

Create a new bead

Usage: br create [OPTIONS] --title <TITLE>

Options:
      --title <TITLE>              Bead title
  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>      Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
      --type <TYPE>                Bead type [default: task]
      --priority <PRIORITY>        Priority (0=Critical, 4=Backlog) [default: 2]
      --description <DESCRIPTION>  Description
      --assignee <ASSIGNEE>        Assignee
      --label <LABEL>              Labels
  -h, --he

args.command:
br create --title "Add pose-detection entry to github-eventsource.yml" --description "Add pose-detection EventSource entry to k8s/iad-ci/argo-events/github-eventsource.yml in jedarden/declarative-config.

Specification:
- EventSource: github-webhooks (ns argo-events)
- owner: jedarden
- names: [pose-detection]
- webhook endpoint: /pose-detection
- port: 12000
- url: https://webhooks-ci.ardenone.com
- events: [push]
- apiToken: from github-webhook-secret (key: token)
- webhookSecret: from github-webhook-secret (key: webhook-secret)
- insecure: false
- active: true

Acceptance criteria:
1. github-eventsource.yml has pose-detection entry under github-webhooks EventSource
2. File is committed and pushed to declarative-config
3. Changes are visible in git log

Use palm-reading entry as a reference pattern." --label split-child
args.description: Create first child bead: eventsource entry

output: bf-4jr

args.command:
br create --title "Create pose-detection-sensor.yml" --description "Create pose-detection-sensor.yml in k8s/iad-ci/argo-events/ in jedarden/declarative-config, modeled on palm-reading-sensor.yml.

Specification:
- Sensor: pose-detection-sensor (ns argo-events)
- Dependency: eventSourceName=github-webhooks, eventName=pose-detection
- Filters:
  * headers.X-Github-Event == push
  * body.ref == refs/heads/main
  * auto-bump cascade guard (exclude ci: auto-bump commits from jedarden)
- Trigger: argoWorkflow submit
  * generateName: pose-detection-build-
  * workflowTemplateRef: pose-detection-build
  * params: git-repo=jedarden/pose-detection, branch=main

Acceptance criteria:
1. pose-detection-sensor.yml created with correct spec
2. File is committed and pushed to declarative-config
3. File exists in repo" --label split-child
args.description: Create second child bead: sensor file

output: bf-1qs

args.description: Add dependency: child 2 depends on child 1

  tip: to pass '--dep-add-blocker' as a value, use '-- --dep-add-blocker'

args.command:
br create --title "Verify Argo Events resources synced" --description "After pushing both github-eventsource.yml and pose-detection-sensor.yml changes, verify that ArgoCD has synced them to the iad-ci cluster.

Acceptance criteria:
1. kubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig get eventsource github-webhooks -n argo-events shows the pose-detection entry
2. kubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig get sensor pose-detection-sensor -n argo-events exists and is Active
3. Both resources are in Ready state (check .status.conditions)

This confirms the argo-events app on iad-ci has synced successfully." --label split-child
args.description: Create third child bead: verify sync

output: bf-4di

Update a bead

Usage: br update [OPTIONS] <ID>

Arguments:
  <ID>  Bead ID

Options:
      --title <TITLE>
          New title
  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>
          Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
      --status <STATUS>
          New status
      --priority <PRIORITY>
          New priority
      --assignee <ASSIGNEE>
          New assignee
      --description <DESCRIPTION>
          New description
      --acceptance-criteria <ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA>
          New

bead-forge - Drop-in replacement for beads_rust (br)

Usage: br [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

Commands:
  create         Create a new bead
  list           List beads
  show           Show bead details
  update         Update a bead
  close          Close a bead
  reopen         Reopen a bead
  delete         Delete a bead
  ready          Show ready (unblocked) beads
  claim          Claim a bead (atomic)
  init           Initialize a new workspace
  sync           Sync (flush to JSONL or import from JS

Manage dependencies

Usage: br dep [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  add     Add a dependency
  remove  Remove a dependency
  list    List dependencies of an issue
  tree    Show dependency tree rooted at issue
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>  Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
  -h, --help                   Print help

Add a dependency

Usage: br dep add [OPTIONS] <BLOCKER>

Arguments:
  <BLOCKER>  Bead that blocks (the bead being depended on)

Options:
      --blocks <BLOCKS>        Bead that is blocked (depends on the blocker)
  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>  Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
  -t, --type <TYPE>            Dependency type (e.g., blocks, relates_to) [default: blocks]
  -h, --help                   Print help
Added dependency: bf-1qs depends on bf-4jr (blocks)

args.command:
br create --title "Verify GitHub webhook creation" --description "After the EventSource syncs, verify that the GitHub webhook was automatically created on the jedarden/pose-detection repository.

The EventSource controller uses the apiToken from github-webhook-secret to create the webhook.

Acceptance criteria:
1. gh api repos/jedarden/pose-detection/hooks shows a webhook for https://webhooks-ci.ardenone.com/pose-detection
2. Webhook is active (active: true)
3. Webhook has push events configured
4. Webhook uses the correct secret

This confirms the EventSource successfully created the GitHub webhook." --label split-child
args.description: Create fourth child bead: verify webhook

output: bf-1xh
Added dependency: bf-4di depends on bf-1qs (blocks)
Added dependency: bf-1xh depends on bf-4di (blocks)

args.command:
br create --title "Test webhook triggers workflow" --description "After all resources are verified, test the end-to-end webhook trigger by making a push to main on the pose-detection repository.

Acceptance criteria:
1. A push to main on jedarden/pose-detection triggers a Workflow run
2. kubectl --kubeconfig=/home/coding/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig get workflows -n argo-workflows shows pose-detection-build-* workflow
3. Workflow has correct parameters (git-repo=jedarden/pose-detection, branch=main)
4. No infinite build loop occurs (auto-bump guard works)

This confirms the complete webhook → sensor → workflow pipeline works." --label split-child
args.description: Create fifth child bead: test trigger

output: bf-4zz
Added dependency: bf-4zz depends on bf-1xh (blocks)
Added dependency: bf-1sg depends on bf-4zz (blocks)

  -w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>  Workspace directory (defaults to current directory's .beads/)
  -h, --help                   Print help
